dna.borrowedTrait: two-column alternating-row service layout with left-anchored oversized service number
dna.divergenceRationale: All three recent own-builds used either centered-classic or warm-editorial archetypes with split-image or centered hero variants — magazine-split with an asymmetric-left hero breaks both dimensions simultaneously. The warm-editorial family was avoided entirely because the brand brief's deep ocean navy palette and grotesque type system (Plus Jakarta Sans) signal editorial-bold territory, not a community-warmth register.
dna.creativeDirection: A coastal authority broadsheet — the page reads like a premium OC lifestyle publication that happens to be a dental practice, with navy-anchored editorial columns, amber accent lines used as thin typographic rules, and Dr. Nguyen's credentials surfaced through pull-quote rhythm rather than bullet-point credential dumps.
dna.writingTone: Warm, clear, specific to Costa Mesa.
dna.brandSummary: OC Healthy Smiles, led by Dr. Mark Nguyen, serving Costa Mesa, — A coastal authority broadsheet — the page reads like a premium OC lifestyle publication that happens to be a dental practice, with navy-anchored editorial columns, amber accent lines used as thin typographic rules, and Dr. Nguyen's credentials surfaced through pull-quote rhythm rather than bullet-point credential dumps.
doRules: Use the archetype's established magazine-split layout — don't mix incompatible grid styles mid-page.
Hero variant must be asymmetric-left — lock this choice.
Card treatment is soft-shadow — apply consistently across all card instances.
Phone number must be a tel: link in the mobile header, minimum 44px touch target.
Doctor name and credentials must appear without scrolling on the homepage.
Primary CTA copy must reference a specific action and ideally the doctor or city.
dontRules: No gradient text (background-clip: text) — AI-slop tell #1.
No faux-glass / backdrop-filter cards unless archetype explicitly calls for it.
No centered-headline + two-CTA + microlinks hero — the most overused AI pattern.
No stat-bar section if yearsExperience/happyPatients data is missing — render empty = fail.
No stock photography of smiling model families.

No generic, boilerplate, or template-sounding copy. Every headline, tagline, and CTA must reference this specific practice, doctor, or city — never industry-generic phrasing that could appear on any competitor site.
No archetype+hero combo already used in recent own-builds.
No section that renders with dashes, nulls, or placeholder text visible to users.
dna.evalRationale: Candidate 1 edges out the others with the most specific and evocative creative direction — 'coastal authority broadsheet' with pull-quote credential rhythm rather than bullet dumps is a genuinely distinctive editorial concept, not just a palette description. Candidate 2's section order (services before doctor-intro) weakens trust-building flow for a credentialed specialist practice, and Candidate 0's language is slightly more generic despite sharing the same structural bones.

candidates.divergenceRationale: All three recent builds used either centered or split-image hero variants inside centered-classic or warm-editorial archetypes — this build deliberately moves into the editorial/bold family with magaz
